summary: This case centers on persistent repetitive worry about climate politics with a background dread, catastrophic future expectations, impaired long-term planning, and attentional capture by worry (C01–C05). The proposed BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers target (1) cognitive-behavioral and metacognitive processes to reduce rumination and distortions, (2) attentional control and worry-management tactics, (3) concrete planning and action initiation, (4) sleep and physical health to support cognitive functioning, (5) social support as a buffer, and (6) values-focused motivation to counter fatalism. Levers are chosen as modifiable predictors with feasible measurement and short-to-medium time horizons to move the observed criteria.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured CBT-based cognitive restructuring with daily thought records and brief reframing of climate/political worry; incorporate evidence-for/likelihood checks and a 5-minute scheduled worry window.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Intrusive repetitive thoughts (C01) and catastrophic future beliefs (C03) are maintained by maladaptive cognitive appraisals; identifying distortions and testing beliefs can reduce frequency and distress.
solutions.measurement: Weekly intrusion frequency and distress (0–10) for C01; belief strength/probability estimates for C03 (0–100%).
solutions.safety_notes: None. Monitor for distress during cognitive challenges; provide crisis resources if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C01, C03; PSYCHO: CBT_Foundations (ID81) and Cognitive_Structuring (ID84).

solutions.solution_candidate: Introduce a 15–30 minute scheduled worry time daily; practice attention redirection to neutral tasks during outside-scheduled worry; use cue-based prompts to resume task-focused activity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured worry time reduces constant cognitive load; attention control training reduces attentional capture by worry.
solutions.measurement: Daily worry time (min); attention performance on brief tasks; self-rated focus during tasks.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C01, C05; PSYCHO: Mindfulness_Based_Interventions (ID170), Attention_Systems_Training (ID92).

solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly planning session to create concrete, if-then plans for future tasks; break goals into small steps; calendar reminders to support initiation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Explicit planning improves initiation and follow-through; reducing cognitive load from long-term goals.
solutions.measurement: Weeks with at least 1 implemented future task; task completion rate; self-rated planning quality.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C04; PSYCHO: Behavior_Change_and_Activation (ID79) and Planning (ID76).

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily 5–10 minute mindfulness/acceptance practice; urge surfing for worries; nonjudgmental present-moment awareness to reduce rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness reduces rumination and affective reactivity, improving attentional control and mood.
solutions.measurement: Daily mindfulness minutes; weekly rumination scale (e.g., RRS); worry duration.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent sleep-wake times, wind-down routine, reduce screen exposure before bed; maintain a sleep diary; optimize sleep environment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep reduces baseline anxiety and cognitive interference, supporting attention and executive control.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary metrics (duration, latency, awakenings); daytime sleepiness; weekly planning quality.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C02; BIO: Sleep_Routines (ID64) and Sleep_Environment (ID62).

solutions.solution_candidate: Engage in 150 minutes/week of moderate aerobic activity (walking/cycling); track with a log; monitor mood and cognition changes.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise reduces anxiety and enhances executive function, reducing cognitive load from worry and aiding attention.
solutions.measurement: Weekly minutes of aerobic activity; mood/anxiety ratings; basic cognitive task performance.

solutions.solution_candidate: Regular brief social check-ins with a trusted person; shared activities; bolster belonging and accountability for future plans.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support buffers negative affect and supports planning; belonging reduces rumination.
solutions.measurement: Weekly social interactions count; perceived support; planning adherence.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C02, C04; SOCIAL: Belonging (ID198) and Social_Linkage (ID185).

solutions.solution_candidate: Values clarification exercises to align actions with long-term goals; develop a values-driven, flexible future plan; enhances motivation and reduces fatalistic thinking.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Values alignment provides purpose, reduces catastrophic thinking, and supports proactive planning.
solutions.measurement: Values alignment score; future orientation scale; number of values-consistent plans per week.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP: C03, C04, C02; PSYCHO: Meaning_Update (ID75) and Values_Framework (ID115).
